LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YAHOO! INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT LLEGED, NOR COULD HE, THAT DEFENDANT IS A STATE ACTOR SUBJECT TO THE FREE SPEECH CLAUSE OF THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS ADEQUATELY PLED. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNCERTAIN AS TO THE PHRASE "INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY" AND THUS PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO CLARIFY THAT UNCERTAINTY. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS TO THE FEDERAL DISTRICT COURT DECISION AND DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING MATTERS. TERMS OF CONDITIONS AND A PRINTOUT FROM A WEBSITE ARE NOT PROPER SUBJECTS OF JUDICIAL NOTICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT YAHOO! INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2012 Set For Trial On Dec-10-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Megan F. Cesare-Eastman, Esq., from K&L Gates, 415-882-8086, for defendant and moving party
Mark S. Buza, Plaintiff and opposing party in Pro Per (no card provided)

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT YAHOO! INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action, overruled as to the third cause of action, and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff has not lleged, nor could he, that defendant is a state actor subject to the free speech clause of the California Constitution. The third cause of action is adequately pled. The fourth cause of action is uncertain as to the phrase "intellectual property" and thus plaintiff is given leave to clarify that uncertainty. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted as to the federal district court decision and denied as to the remaining matters. Terms of conditions and a printout from a website are not proper subjects of judicial notice. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-23-2011 Off Calendar
